 {"id":"bd-8sho","title":"bd-1j4 subtask: Hard-parts docs/parity/support-matrix truth reconciliation","description":"Background:
- Hard-parts completion claims require synchronized docs/support matrix/feature parity narratives.

Goal:
- Reconcile hard-parts implementation reality with user-facing claims and machine-generated reports.

Deliverables:
1) Hard-parts status truth table across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality report.
2) Drift guard updates for hard-parts claim consistency.
3) Explicit “deferred vs implemented” rationale for each hard-parts subsystem.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No contradictory hard-parts claims across docs/artifacts.
- Drift gates catch future inconsistency automatically.

Verification & Logging:
- Drift guard tests and fixtures.
- Structured logs for reconciliation checks and mismatches.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedMaple","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:33.576518493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:20.345066285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:20.345031921Z","close_reason":"Completed hard-parts truth table reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report with new drift guard script, harness test, and CI wiring.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","drift","hard-parts","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1063,"issue_id":"bd-8sho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedMaple claiming bd-8sho and starting hard-parts truth reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report.\n\nPlanned deliverables:\n1)  artifact with per-subsystem implemented vs deferred rationale.\n2)  drift guard script.\n3)  guard test.\n4) doc updates in  and  to include explicit hard-parts truth lines tied to the artifact.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:48:02Z"},{"id":1064,"issue_id":"bd-8sho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (RedMaple): completed hard-parts docs/parity/support/reality truth reconciliation with drift guard wiring.\n\nImplemented:\n- New canonical artifact: tests/conformance/hard_parts_truth_table.v1.json\n  - per-subsystem status + implemented/deferred scope rationale for startup/threading/resolver/nss/locale/iconv\n  - support_matrix expectations per subsystem\n  - reality_report snapshot binding + contradiction summary\n- New drift guard: scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n  - validates artifact schema, README + FEATURE_PARITY hard-parts truth lines, support_matrix expectations, and reality_report snapshot alignment\n  - emits structured JSON event with trace_id/mode/api_family/outcome/artifact refs\n- New harness test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/hard_parts_truth_test.rs\n  - artifact schema validation\n  - guard script execution gate\n- Docs reconciled:\n  - README.md hard-parts truth section added\n  - FEATURE_PARITY.md hard-parts truth section added\n- CI wiring:\n  - scripts/ci.sh now runs scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n\nVerification executed (PASS):\n- scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test hard_parts_truth_test\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test parity_report_drift_test\n\nNote:\n- /tmp is full on this host; verification used CARGO_TARGET_DIR under /data/tmp for deterministic local runs.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:17Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8zsp","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for inet IP parsers","description":"parse_ipv4, parse_ipv6, inet_pton, inet_aton in frankenlibc_core::inet are classic security-sensitive parsers. Add Archetype-1 smoke-fuzz proptests: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) output bounded, (c) format-roundtrip for inputs that parse successfully (parse(format(x)) == x). Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:29:16.512627925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.178725431Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.176686404Z","close_reason":"Added 8 inet smoke-fuzz proptests in commit 0cf34de: parse_ipv4 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), parse_ipv6 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), inet_pton (af-cube), inet_aton (legacy). ~4100 case evaluations per run, no panics found on first sweep. Covers historical CVE surface for dotted-quad arithmetic and IPv6 '::' expansion.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-95tq","title":"resolv: gai_cancel and gai_suspend empty requests should return EAI_ALLDONE","description":"Concrete regression: host glibc returns EAI_ALLDONE with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate async DNS calls like gai_cancel(NULL), gai_cancel(&zeroed_gaicb), gai_suspend(NULL, 0, timeout), and gai_suspend([NULL], 1, timeout). FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM with ENOSYS. Restore host-parity degenerate semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.765525235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:19:52.577129952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:19:52.574907623Z","close_reason":"Restored host-parity EAI_ALLDONE semantics for synchronous-degenerate gai_cancel/gai_suspend inputs and verified locally plus via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-95zf","title":"AI_ADDRCONFIG review: null-node IPv6-only and dual-stack coverage","description":"Review of cod's bd-4qqu fix (commit 8996ad1 hoisting AI_ADDRCONFIG filter out of Some(text) branch) found 2 coverage gaps in the null-node test set: (1) IPv6-only interface state — the null-node IPv4 filter case is tested but the symmetric IPv6-only case is not, so an asymmetric filter bug would escape. (2) dual-stack interface state with null-node — no test verifies both AF_INET and AF_INET6 nodes are preserved when both families have non-loopback routes. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/resolv_abi_test.rs additions only, no code changes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:37:35.826818907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:38:54.778398472Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:38:54.776390943Z","close_reason":"Added 2 complementary null-node AI_ADDRCONFIG tests in commit b935235: IPv6-only filter symmetry + dual-stack preservation. Suite now covers all 4 quadrants of the {IPv4,IPv6} x {filter,keep} matrix for null-node under AI_ADDRCONFIG.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-faew","title":"Exit-time segfault in LD_PRELOAD mode","notes":"ROOT CAUSE FOUND: glibc's internal _IO_list_all not patched during LD_PRELOAD. Binaries with COPY relocations work; others crash. glibc's exit path (_IO_flush_all_lockp) iterates its own _IO_list_all with corrupted vtables. FIX: dlvsym glibc's _IO_list_all at startup and set to NULL or our valid list. Confirmed _exit bypasses crash, main-return crashes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-17T23:45:37.604034170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T00:42:28.505593885Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T00:42:28.501510444Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-g7ux","title":"inet_addr returns host-order u32 instead of network-order","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T10:10:37.756487821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:27:27.384420744Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:27:27.384352917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","inet"],"comments":[{"id":1086,"issue_id":"bd-g7ux","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"frankenlibc_core::inet::inet_addr uses u32::from_be_bytes(octets) which returns the host-endian integer value (0x7F000001 = 2130706433 for 127.0.0.1). glibc inet_addr returns the same 4 bytes stored as a native u32 in network byte order (0x0100007F = 16777343 on LE). Fix: change from_be_bytes to from_ne_bytes in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs:63. Detected by conformance differential execution.","created_at":"2026-02-13T10:10:45Z"},{"id":1087,"issue_id":"bd-g7ux","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"Fixed inet_addr to return network byte order. Changed u32::from_be_bytes to u32::from_ne_bytes in frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs:63. Updated 5 tests that incorrectly used to_be_bytes() to use to_ne_bytes(). Also fixed pre-existing pthread compilation error: added tls_values field to ThreadHandle and wired up register_thread_tls call with the correct 2-arg signature. All 61 inet tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:27:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ta3b","title":"C.1: Implement gshadow line parser in frankenlibc-core","description":"## Goal
Add a gshadow line parser to frankenlibc-core, following the exact pattern of the existing /etc/passwd parser in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/mod.rs.

## Reference: Existing Pattern
The passwd parser at pwd/mod.rs uses:
- `pub struct Passwd { pw_name: Vec<u8>, pw_passwd: Vec<u8>, ... }`
- `pub fn parse_passwd_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<Passwd>` — splits on colons, validates field count
- `pub fn lookup_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<Passwd>` — iterates lines, matches name

The gshadow parser should follow this EXACT pattern for consistency.

## gshadow File Format (/etc/gshadow)
Each line: `group_name:encrypted_password:administrators:members`
- group_name: non-empty, no colons (same as /etc/group name field)
- encrypted_password: may be empty (""), or "!" (locked), or "*" (no password)
- administrators: comma-separated usernames (may be empty — no admins)
- members: comma-separated usernames (may be empty — no members)

Example lines:
  root:*::
  shadow:!::
  sudo:!::alice,bob
  dev:x:alice:alice,bob,charlie

## Implementation

### File: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/gshadow.rs (NEW FILE)
Add `pub mod gshadow;` to crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/mod.rs.

### Struct
```rust
pub struct Gshadow {
    pub sg_namp: Vec<u8>,    // group name
    pub sg_passwd: Vec<u8>,  // encrypted password
    pub sg_adm: Vec<u8>,     // raw comma-separated admin list
    pub sg_mem: Vec<u8>,     // raw comma-separated member list
}
```

### Functions
```rust
pub fn parse_gshadow_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<Gshadow>
pub fn lookup_gshadow_by_name(content: &[u8], name: &[u8]) -> Option<Gshadow>
```

### Parsing Logic
1. Skip empty lines and lines starting with #
2. Split on b':' — must have exactly 4 fields
3. Field 0 (name) must be non-empty
4. Fields 1-3 may be empty
5. Return Gshadow with owned Vec<u8> copies

## Inline Tests (#[cfg(test)])
- parse_valid_full_line: `sudo:!:admin1,admin2:user1,user2` -> all 4 fields correct
- parse_minimal_line: `root:*::` -> password="*", admins="", members=""
- parse_empty_password: `grp:::` -> password is empty
- parse_locked_password: `grp:!::` -> password is "!"
- parse_with_admins_and_members: `dev:x:alice:alice,bob` -> admins="alice", members="alice,bob"
- reject_too_few_fields: `root:*:` (3 colons needed, only 2) -> None
- reject_empty_name: `:*::` -> None
- reject_comment_line: `# comment` -> None
- reject_empty_line: "" -> None
- lookup_finds_entry: content with 3 lines, lookup "sudo" finds correct one
- lookup_returns_none_for_missing: lookup "nonexistent" returns None
- lookup_is_case_sensitive: "ROOT" does not match "root"

## Acceptance Criteria
Parser handles all gshadow field combinations. 12 inline tests pass. cargo clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:37.066192744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:56.720091402Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:56.719809839Z","close_reason":"Implemented gshadow parser in frankenlibc-core/src/pwd/gshadow.rs. Struct Gshadow with sg_namp/sg_passwd/sg_adm/sg_mem fields. parse_gshadow_line() handles comments, blank lines, field validation. lookup_gshadow_by_name() scans content for case-sensitive name match. 14 inline tests pass covering: valid full/minimal lines, empty/locked password, admin+member lists, reject malformed, lookup hit/miss/case sensitivity.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pwd","stub-elimination"]}
 {"id":"bd-tbhb","title":"scan_wordexp_syntax misses bad characters inside parameter expansion","description":"`scan_wordexp_syntax` in `unistd_abi.rs` skips all character validation when `parameter_brace_depth > 0`. This means unquoted bad characters (like `|`, `&`, `;`) inside a brace expansion (e.g. `${foo|bar}`) will go undetected, and `wordexp` will return `0` instead of `WRDE_BADCHAR`, breaking parity with glibc.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:35:07.847147776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:00:20.086037646Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T21:59:50.078157645Z","close_reason":"Fixed missing bad character detection inside brace expansion in scan_wordexp_syntax.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:14:04.095783748Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:14:38.179939757Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-thm2","title":"Fix c11threads managed-attr/host-pthread init mismatch","description":"c11threads_abi::mtx_init was building a FrankenLibC-managed pthread mutexattr word and passing it into host pthread_mutex_init, which triggered a glibc __pthread_tpp_change_priority assertion in c11threads_abi_test. Fix the C11 mutex/condvar init lane so C11 synchronization objects are initialized consistently on the native-managed path, then verify the focused C11 suite and workspace ABI checks via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilentFrog","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:34:41.394889788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204943932Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:54.204703667Z","close_reason":"Resolved C11 mutex/condvar init mismatch by keeping C11 synchronization initialization on the native-managed lane; focused c11threads tests now pass and workspace tests advance to unrelated dlfcn failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1358,"issue_id":"bd-thm2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented scoped native-mutex delegation helpers in pthread_abi and switched c11threads mutex/condvar init to use the native-managed lane. Verification: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test test_mtx_init_lock_unlock_destroy -- --exact --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed; `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test c11threads_abi_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` passed (36/36); `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` passed; `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed; `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` completed without diffs.  {"id":"bd-yktn","title":"EPIC: Eliminate all remaining stub/placeholder functions (12 gaps across 4 tracks)","description":"## Background

A comprehensive audit of the entire FrankenLibC codebase identified 12 remaining functions that are stubs or placeholders returning hardcoded defaults instead of performing real work. These 12 are distinct from the ~100 INTENTIONAL stubs (deprecated syscalls, legacy RPC, profiling hooks) that correctly return ENOSYS/-1/null by design.

## Scope: 12 Gaps in 4 Tracks

### Track A (bd-di5w): _IO_iter_* stream iterators — 4 functions [P2]
Location: io_internal_abi.rs:1430-1457. Currently return null. Should iterate NativeStreamRegistry.

### Track B (bd-olgi): dlinfo — 1 function [P2]
Location: glibc_internal_abi.rs:4290-4297. Currently returns ENOSYS. Should handle RTLD_DI_LINKMAP and RTLD_DI_ORIGIN.

### Track C (bd-osb8 -> bd-ta3b, bd-kcbm): gshadow — 8 functions [P3]
Location: pwd_abi.rs:969-1040. Currently return null/ENOENT. Should parse /etc/gshadow.

### Track D (bd-9dq5): gai_* async DNS — 3 functions [P3]
Location: unistd_abi.rs:17404-17423. Currently return ENOSYS incorrectly. Should return correct POSIX error codes for synchronous-only resolver.

## Execution Strategy
Ordered by impact-to-effort: A (30min) > B (45min) > D (20min) > C (90min).
Tracks A, B, and D are independent leaf tasks. Track C has sequential subtasks (C.1 parser -> C.2 wiring).
All 4 tracks can be parallelized across agents.

## Verification Gate (applies to ALL tracks)
1. Each track must include unit tests: positive path, negative path, edge cases, error conditions
2. Each track must include an e2e test script under scripts/
3. All tests must emit structured JSONL logs with: trace_id, mode, api_family, symbol, decision_path, errno, latency_ns
4. cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings must remain clean after each track
5. cargo fmt --check must pass
6. LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (58 passes) must not regress
7. Each track's e2e script must pass in both strict and hardened modes where applicable

## Non-goals
~100 intentional ENOSYS/no-op stubs for deprecated/removed/BSD-only syscalls, profiling hooks, and legacy RPC are NOT in scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
All 12 stub functions replaced with real implementations or correct error returns. All unit tests pass. All e2e scripts pass. Workspace clippy clean. Smoke suite green.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:16:49.953403474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:53:15.109564147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:53:15.109141516Z","close_reason":"ALL 4 tracks closed:
- Track A (bd-di5w): _IO_iter_* implemented over NativeStreamRegistry (8 tests)
- Track B (bd-olgi): dlinfo for RTLD_DI_LINKMAP/ORIGIN via /proc/self/maps (clippy clean)
- Track C (bd-osb8): gshadow parsing with core parser + ABI wiring (14+34 tests)
- Track D (bd-9dq5): gai_cancel/error/suspend with correct POSIX error codes (not ENOSYS)

12 stub functions eliminated. 22 new tests added. All clippy clean. LD_PRELOAD smoke passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","completeness","stub-elimination"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yktn","depends_on_id":"bd-9dq5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:29.490486574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yktn","depends_on_id":"bd-di5w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:22.530662824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yktn","depends_on_id":"bd-olgi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:57.066121780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yktn","depends_on_id":"bd-osb8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:22.214472130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-yos","title":"Thread bootstrap: minimal pthread_create/join/detach path with TLS handoff","description":"Critique mapping: #3 + #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement a minimal working pthread_create path (clone-based bootstrap acceptable for phase 1).
- Join/detach semantics, thread exit value propagation, TLS init handoff.

Acceptance:
- Smoke suite binary using pthread_create/join runs clean in strict+hardened.
- Top-stub list no longer includes pthread_create in phase-1 target set.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.465414726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:12:53.473663741Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:12:53.472570984Z","close_reason":"Acceptance met: pthread strict+hardened smoke + updated top-stub ranking evidence recorded","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","pthread","tls"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yos","depends_on_id":"bd-18rq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yos","depends_on_id":"bd-1f35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yos","depends_on_id":"bd-3hud","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yos","depends_on_id":"bd-mjon","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yos","depends_on_id":"bd-rth1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1228,"issue_id":"bd-yos","author":"Codex","text":"Codex here.  {"id":"bd-ypst","title":"stdio_abi_test exits SIGSEGV after passing filtered tests","description":"Filtered stdio ABI tests report their test body as ok, then the test binary exits with signal 11. This masks real conformance results and likely relates to stdio/global teardown or exported FILE aliases. Investigate and fix without disturbing existing stdio work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:20:55.593063778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:32:24.124461601Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:32:24.122395993Z","close_reason":"Fixed stdio ABI teardown SIGSEGV by patching host libio exit-time list before stdio registry initialization; verified via rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdio_abi_test (99 passed, 25 ignored), rch cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests, rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --tests -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check --package frankenlibc-abi, and git diff --check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","conformance","stdio","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1417,"issue_id":"bd-ypst","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"GDB evidence: filtered stdio_abi_test bodies pass, then process SIGSEGVs in glibc __GI__IO_flush_all during _IO_cleanup/__run_exit_handlers. Backtrace points to glibc exit-time FILE list cleanup rather than test assertion failure, likely interaction with exported stdio globals / _IO_2_1_* aliases or _IO_list_all patching.","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:21:58Z"}]}
